chat.text: Now that we've shipped the SCONE draft let's figure out what it means

chat.author: Qin Wu
chat.text: @Tim Chow, we can not hear you, can you share your comments in this chat window

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: I thought the P(two SCONE NEs) -> 0?

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: P(SCONE) ~ 0 right now, but we'll see how things play out.

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: @MT I remember a whole conversation on the "no guidance" codepoint could still work even if NEs don't always wake up, because there wouldn't be a 2-NE situation?

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: MT: how likely is scone flow metadata to be exposed visibly to the user

chat.author: Martin Thomson
chat.text: I don't know how this will play out, but the option exists.

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: is anyone working on that kind of visibility or instrumentation to detect these kinds of shenanigans?

chat.author: Brian Trammell
chat.text: i expect the folks who do measurement in this space will do so once you actually get scone-labelled packets.

chat.author: Daniel Gillmor
chat.text: it'd be nice to hear from those folks!

chat.author: Anoop Tomar
chat.text: lets not delay protocol doc due to appman

chat.author: Anoop Tomar
chat.text: There is a real urgency for protocol doc to move to next stage.

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: +1 Anoop

chat.author: Sanjay Mishra
chat.text: I believe Appman is in a good shape and with about 8 issues open with 6 PRs so I would like to see the goal of closure here to not hold back Appman either

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: Comment for Martin D.: there are privacy issues. But there are also trust issues. A misbehaving client would be able to "echo" data rates that have very little correlation with what the network said.

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: thanks Christian. I think that's also possible with classic SCONE, maybe more so because apps are generally less responsible than transports (?). But I'd love for you to think on it, and decide if it's not a real problem, fixable, or fatally flawed.

chat.author: Gorry Fairhurst
chat.text: As AD, I heard that the WG thought that both deliverables were mature - and that there was no objection to the proposal to not further delay the protocol (unless someone else has concerns).
